column2:
Academia is like a big world of learning and teaching, where people study different subjects to discover new things and share knowledge with others. Imagine a place where teachers, students, and researchers come together to explore everything from science and history to art and technology. In academia, people might work in schools, colleges, or universities, and their job is to learn about their favorite topics, conduct experiments, write papers, and teach others what they know. It's a bit like being a detective, always curious and eager to find out more about how the world works.

In academia, there are many important skills that can be useful in other areas, like tech. For example, active listening helps you understand what others are saying, which is crucial when working on a team. Adaptability means being flexible and open to change, which is important when new challenges arise. Creativity and critical thinking help you come up with new ideas and solve problems, while written and verbal communication are key for sharing your thoughts clearly. Collaboration and relationship building are about working well with others, and organization helps you keep track of your tasks. All these skills are valuable in tech and many other fields, making academia a great place to develop abilities that can be used in lots of different jobs.
column3:
Here are some examples of job positions in this industry:

As a professor, you get to dive deep into your favorite subject and share your passion with students. Imagine standing in front of a classroom, explaining fascinating concepts and sparking curiosity in young minds. You also get to conduct research, exploring new ideas and contributing to the world’s knowledge.

As a researcher, you're a detective of the academic world. You spend your days investigating questions that nobody has answered yet. Whether it's in a lab, a library, or out in the field, you gather data, conduct experiments, and write papers to share your discoveries with others.

As a librarian, you become the guardian of knowledge. You help students and teachers find the information they need, organize books and digital resources, and even teach people how to use technology to access information. It's a job that combines a love for books with the excitement of helping others learn.

column2:
The accounting industry is all about keeping track of money and making sure everything adds up correctly. Imagine if you had a big jar of candy, and you wanted to know exactly how many pieces you have, how many you give away, and how many you get from friends. Accountants do something similar but with money. They help businesses and people understand where their money is going, how much they have, and how they can save or spend it wisely. This is important because it helps companies make smart decisions about their finances, like when to buy new equipment or hire more people.

In the accounting industry, there are many soft skills that are really useful and can be used in other jobs too. For example, active listening is important because accountants need to understand what their clients need. Attention to detail is crucial because even a small mistake in numbers can cause big problems. Critical thinking and decision-making help accountants solve problems and make smart choices. Organization and time management are key because accountants often have to handle lots of tasks and deadlines. These skills, along with good communication and relationship building, are valuable in many other careers, not just accounting.
column3:
Here are some examples of job positions in this industry:

As a bookkeeper, you get to be the person who keeps track of all the money coming in and going out of a business. It's like being the scorekeeper in a game, making sure every dollar is counted and recorded correctly. You help businesses know exactly how much money they have and what they can spend.

As a tax preparer, you help people and companies figure out how much they owe in taxes or how much they might get back. It's like solving a puzzle where you put all the pieces together to make sure everything is correct. You get to learn about different rules and help people save money by finding deductions they might not know about.

As a financial analyst, you get to look at numbers and trends to help businesses make smart decisions about their money. It's like being a fortune teller for finances, predicting what might happen in the future based on the data you have. You help companies decide where to invest their money and how to grow their business.

column2:
The agriculture industry is all about growing food and raising animals to provide us with the things we eat and use every day. Imagine a big farm where people plant seeds, take care of crops, and look after animals like cows and chickens. Farmers work hard to make sure we have fresh fruits, vegetables, milk, and meat. They use tools and machines to help them, and they have to pay attention to the weather and seasons to know the best time to plant and harvest. Agriculture is super important because it helps feed the world and keeps us healthy.

In agriculture, people develop many soft skills that are useful in other jobs too. For example, adaptability is important because farmers need to adjust to changes in weather or market demands. Attention to detail helps them notice small changes in their crops or animals that might need care. Collaboration and teamwork are key because farmers often work together to get big tasks done, like harvesting a field. Critical thinking and decision-making are used when choosing the best crops to plant or how to care for animals. Time management and organization help farmers plan their day efficiently to get everything done. These skills are valuable in any job because they help people work well with others, solve problems, and manage their time effectively.
column3:
Here are some examples of job positions in this industry:

As a crop farmer, you get to work outdoors planting and growing fruits and vegetables. You’ll learn how to use cool machines like tractors to help plant seeds and harvest crops. It's like being a gardener, but on a much larger scale, and you play a big part in making sure people have fresh food to eat.

As a livestock caretaker, you’ll spend your days looking after animals like cows, chickens, and sheep. You’ll feed them, make sure they’re healthy, and even help collect things like milk and eggs. It’s a bit like having a pet, but with a whole farm full of animals that provide important products for everyone.

As an agricultural technician, you’ll use science and technology to help farmers grow more food. You might test soil to see what plants will grow best or use drones to check on crops from the sky. It’s a job that combines nature with the latest tech, making farming more efficient and sustainable.

column2:
The business industry is all about creating, selling, and managing products or services to meet people's needs and wants. Imagine a lemonade stand: you decide what flavors to sell, how much to charge, and how to let people know about your stand. In the business world, people work in different roles to make sure everything runs smoothly, from coming up with new ideas to making sure customers are happy. Businesses can be small, like a local bakery, or huge, like a company that makes smartphones. The goal is to make a profit while providing something valuable to customers.

In the business industry, there are many soft skills that are important and can be used in other areas, like tech. For example, active listening helps you understand what customers or team members need, while adaptability allows you to handle changes and new challenges. Collaboration and teamwork are crucial because working well with others can lead to better ideas and solutions. Problem-solving and critical thinking help you find the best ways to overcome obstacles. Skills like time management and organization ensure that tasks are completed efficiently. These skills are not only useful in business but also in many other fields, making them valuable no matter what career path you choose.
column3:
Here are some examples of job positions in this industry:

As a product manager, you get to be the mastermind behind making sure everything a company sells is top-notch. Imagine being the taste tester for a lemonade stand, ensuring every cup is delicious and refreshing. You work with different teams to improve products and make sure customers love them.

As a financial analyst, you are like the money wizard of the business world. You help companies figure out how to make more money and spend it wisely. It's like keeping track of how much money your lemonade stand makes and finding ways to save on lemons and sugar to boost profits.

As a marketing specialist, you are the creative genius who comes up with fun and exciting ways to attract customers. Think of designing cool posters or creating catchy slogans to get more friends to buy your lemonade. Your job is to make sure everyone knows about the awesome things your company offers.

column2:
The construction industry is all about building things like houses, schools, bridges, and even skyscrapers. Imagine playing with building blocks or LEGO sets, but on a much larger scale! People in this industry work together to create the structures we see around us every day. They use tools and machines to put together materials like wood, steel, and concrete to make sure everything is safe and sturdy. Construction workers, architects, and engineers all play important roles in making sure the buildings are designed well and built to last.

In the construction industry, there are many soft skills that are important and can be used in other areas too. For example, attention to detail is crucial because even a small mistake can cause big problems. Organization helps keep projects on track, while decision-making and critical thinking are needed to solve unexpected challenges. Problem-solving is key when things don't go as planned, and active listening ensures everyone understands their tasks. Collaboration and teamwork are essential because building something big requires many people working together. Verbal communication helps everyone share ideas clearly, and time management ensures projects are completed on schedule. Perseverance is important when things get tough, and logical thinking helps in planning and executing tasks. Interpersonal dynamics and delivering and receiving feedback help maintain a positive work environment. All these skills are valuable in many other jobs too!
column3:
Here are some examples of job positions in this industry:

As a carpenter, you get to work with wood to build things like walls, floors, and roofs. It's like being a master of puzzles, fitting pieces together to create something amazing. Carpenters use tools like hammers and saws to shape and assemble wood, making sure everything is just right for a sturdy and beautiful structure.

Being a crane operator is like playing with a giant claw machine! You get to control a huge crane to lift and move heavy materials like steel beams and concrete blocks. It's an important job because you help put the big pieces in place to build tall buildings and bridges. Plus, you get a bird's-eye view of the construction site from high up in the crane cab.

As an electrician, you bring buildings to life by installing the wiring that powers lights, computers, and all the gadgets we use every day. It's like being a real-life superhero, making sure everything has the electricity it needs to work. Electricians carefully connect wires and install outlets, switches, and lighting fixtures, ensuring everything is safe and functional.

column2:
The creative industry is all about using imagination and original ideas to make things that entertain, inspire, or inform people. This can include making movies, designing video games, writing books, creating art, or even developing new fashion trends. People in this industry use their creativity to come up with new concepts and bring them to life in ways that capture people's attention and emotions. It's like being a professional dreamer who turns ideas into reality, whether it's through a painting, a song, or a cool new app.

In the creative industry, many soft skills are important and can be used in other areas too. For example, active listening helps you understand what others want or need, which is crucial when working on a team project. Adaptability is key because trends and technologies change quickly, so being able to adjust is important. Storytelling is another valuable skill, as it helps you communicate ideas in a way that engages and captivates an audience. Teamwork and collaboration are essential because creating something amazing often involves working with others. These skills, along with creativity and problem-solving, are not only useful in the creative industry but can also be applied to many other fields, making them highly valuable.
column3:
Here are some examples of job positions in this industry:

As a graphic designer, you get to use your artistic skills to create eye-catching visuals for things like posters, websites, and advertisements. It's like being a digital artist where you can play with colors, shapes, and fonts to make something that grabs people's attention and tells a story.

As a music producer, you work behind the scenes to help artists record their songs. They mix different sounds and instruments to create the perfect track. It's like being a chef, but instead of ingredients, you mix beats and melodies to make a hit song.

As a screenwriter, you write the scripts for movies and TV shows. They create the dialogue and plot that actors bring to life on screen. It's like writing a story that millions of people will watch and enjoy, making them laugh, cry, or sit on the edge of their seats.

The development industry is all about creating and improving things, like apps, websites, and software, to make our lives easier and more fun. Imagine the people who design your favorite video games or the apps you use to chat with friends. They work in development! This industry involves a lot of creativity and problem-solving, as developers need to figure out how to make things work smoothly and look great. It's like being a digital builder, where you use your skills to construct amazing virtual worlds and tools.

In the development industry, there are many soft skills that are important and can be used in other areas too. For example, active listening is crucial because developers need to understand what users want and need. Adaptability is key because technology is always changing, and developers must keep up with new trends. Collaboration and teamwork are essential because developers often work in teams to create projects. Problem-solving and critical thinking help developers find solutions to technical challenges. These skills, along with others like time management and communication, are valuable in any job, not just in development. They help you work well with others, manage your time effectively, and think strategically to achieve your goals.
column3:
Here are some examples of job positions in this industry:

As a software developer, you get to create cool apps and games that people use every day! Imagine designing a new game where players can explore magical worlds or an app that helps people learn new skills. You write the code that makes these ideas come to life, turning imagination into reality.

Being a web developer is like being an architect for the internet. You build websites that are not only fun to visit but also easy to use. Whether it's a site for sharing videos, shopping online, or learning new things, you make sure everything looks great and works smoothly, just like a digital playground.

As a mobile app developer, you focus on creating apps for smartphones and tablets. Think about all the apps you use to chat with friends, play music, or even track your fitness goals. You design and build these handy tools, making sure they work perfectly on different devices so people can enjoy them anytime, anywhere.

column2:
The film industry is all about creating movies and shows that entertain, inspire, and tell stories. It's like a big team project where lots of people work together to make something amazing. There are directors who guide the vision, actors who bring characters to life, and writers who create the story. There are also people who handle the cameras, design the sets, and edit the final product. It's a bit like putting together a giant puzzle, where everyone has a special piece to contribute. Whether it's a thrilling action movie or a heartwarming drama, the film industry is all about bringing stories to the screen for everyone to enjoy.

In the film industry, there are many soft skills that are important and can be used in other areas too. For example, active listening is crucial because everyone needs to understand each other's ideas and feedback to make the best movie possible. Adaptability is key because things can change quickly on a film set, and being able to adjust is important. Creativity is at the heart of making films, as it helps in coming up with new ideas and solving problems. Teamwork and collaboration are essential because making a film is a group effort, and everyone needs to work well together. Storytelling is another important skill, as it helps in crafting engaging narratives that capture the audience's attention. These skills are not only useful in making films but can also be applied to many other jobs and situations in life.
column3:
Here are some examples of job positions in this industry:

As a director, you get to be the captain of the ship, guiding the entire team to bring a movie to life. You make important decisions about how the story is told, work closely with actors to shape their performances, and collaborate with everyone to ensure the film looks and feels just right. It's like being the leader of a creative orchestra!

Being a cinematographer is like being the eyes of the film. You get to play with cameras and lighting to create beautiful and dramatic scenes that capture the audience's attention. It's your job to make sure every shot looks amazing, whether it's a breathtaking landscape or an intense close-up of an actor's face.

As a film editor, you are the puzzle master who pieces together all the different scenes to create a seamless story. You work with the director to choose the best takes, add special effects, and make sure the movie flows smoothly. It's like crafting a giant jigsaw puzzle where every piece needs to fit perfectly to tell an engaging story.

The finance industry is all about managing money and helping people and businesses make smart financial decisions. Imagine it like a big puzzle where people work together to figure out the best ways to save, spend, and invest money. People in finance might work in banks, help companies plan their budgets, or give advice on how to save for college or buy a house. It's like being a money detective, where you use clues to help others make the best choices with their money.

In the finance industry, there are many important soft skills that can be used in other areas too. For example, active listening is crucial because you need to understand what people need and want. Adaptability helps you adjust to new financial trends or unexpected changes. Attention to detail is key when dealing with numbers and making sure everything adds up correctly. Teamwork and collaboration are important because you often work with others to solve problems. Critical thinking and decision-making help you analyze situations and choose the best path forward. These skills are not just useful in finance; they can help you succeed in many different jobs and situations in life.
column3:
Here are some examples of job positions in this industry:

As a bank teller, you get to work in a bank and help people with their money. You might help someone deposit their allowance into their savings account or give them cash when they need it. It's like being a friendly face that helps people manage their money safely.

Being a financial advisor is like being a money coach. You help families and individuals plan for their future, whether it's saving up for a big vacation, college, or even retirement. You get to give advice on how to make the most out of their money so they can achieve their dreams.

If you become an investment analyst, you get to dive into the world of stocks and bonds. It's like being a detective, where you research and analyze different companies to help people decide where to invest their money. Your job is to find the best opportunities to help money grow over time.

The government industry is all about running a country and making sure everything works smoothly for its people. Imagine it like a big team that takes care of important things like schools, roads, and safety. People in the government work on creating laws, helping communities, and making decisions that affect everyone. It's like being part of a giant group project where everyone has a role to play, from planning events to solving problems that help make life better for everyone in the country.

In the government industry, there are many soft skills that are important and can be used in other areas, like tech. For example, attention to detail is crucial because government workers need to make sure everything is correct, just like when you're double-checking your homework. Conflict resolution and critical thinking help them solve disagreements and think through tough problems, which is useful in any job. Decision-making and time management are key because they often have to make choices that affect many people and manage their time well to get everything done. Skills like verbal and written communication are important for explaining ideas clearly, while relationship building and interpersonal dynamics help them work well with others. These skills are like tools that help you succeed in any job, including tech, where you might need to solve problems, work with a team, and communicate your ideas effectively.
column3:
Here are some examples of job positions in this industry:

As a police officer, you get to help keep your community safe. It's like being a real-life superhero, where you solve problems, help people in need, and make sure everyone follows the rules. You might patrol neighborhoods, respond to emergencies, or even help find lost pets!

Being a teacher in the government sector means you get to inspire and educate the next generation. Imagine having a classroom full of curious minds eager to learn about the world. You can teach them everything from math to history, and help them discover their passions and talents.

Working as a city planner is like being an architect for your town. You get to design and organize how a city looks and functions. This means planning where parks, schools, and roads go, making sure everything is in the right place so people can live happily and comfortably. It's like building a giant puzzle that everyone gets to enjoy!

The hospitality industry is all about making people feel welcome and comfortable when they are away from home. This includes places like hotels, restaurants, and even theme parks. Imagine going on vacation and staying at a hotel where the staff greets you with a smile, helps you with your luggage, and makes sure your room is cozy and clean. Or think about eating at a restaurant where the waiter is friendly and makes sure you have everything you need to enjoy your meal. People working in hospitality are like friendly hosts who want to make sure you have a great experience, whether you're traveling, dining out, or just having fun.

In the hospitality industry, there are many important soft skills that can be useful in other areas, like tech. For example, active listening is crucial because it helps workers understand what guests need and want. Adaptability is also key, as things can change quickly, and being able to adjust is important. Teamwork and collaboration are essential because working together helps solve problems and create a better experience for everyone. Attention to detail ensures that everything is perfect, from the cleanliness of a room to the presentation of a dish. These skills, along with others like communication and relationship building, are valuable in any job because they help people work well with others and create positive experiences.
column3:
Here are some examples of job positions in this industry:

As a hotel receptionist, you are the friendly face that greets guests when they arrive. You get to check them in, answer their questions, and help them feel at home. It's like being the host of a big party where everyone is excited to be there!

Being a chef in a hotel or restaurant means you get to create delicious meals for guests to enjoy. You can experiment with different recipes and make food that looks as good as it tastes. It's like being a food artist, making sure every dish is a masterpiece!

As a housekeeper, you ensure that guests have a clean and comfortable place to stay. You tidy up rooms, make beds, and add special touches that make guests feel special. It's like being a secret superhero, making everything perfect without anyone noticing!

Industrial design is all about creating and improving products that we use every day, like smartphones, furniture, or even cars. Imagine someone who loves to draw and build things, using their creativity to design cool new gadgets or make existing ones better. People in this industry think about how things look, feel, and work, making sure they are not only useful but also enjoyable to use. It's like being an inventor who focuses on making life easier and more fun by designing things that people will love to use.

In industrial design, many soft skills are important and can be used in other areas too. For example, active listening and collaboration are crucial because designers often work in teams and need to understand what others are saying to create the best products. Creativity and problem-solving help them come up with new ideas and fix any issues that arise. Attention to detail ensures that every part of a design is just right, while adaptability allows designers to adjust their plans when things change. These skills are valuable in many jobs, not just in industrial design, because they help people work well with others and come up with great solutions to challenges.

Here are some examples of job positions in this industry:

As a product designer, you get to dream up and sketch out new ideas for everyday items like backpacks or headphones. You think about how these products should look and feel, making sure they're both cool and practical for people to use.

As a furniture designer, you create stylish and comfy chairs, tables, and sofas. You get to choose materials and colors, making sure each piece not only looks great but also fits perfectly in homes and offices.

As a car designer, you design the sleek shapes and interiors of cars, making them not only fast and efficient but also super stylish. You get to combine creativity with technology to create vehicles that people love to drive.

The legal industry is all about understanding and applying the rules and laws that help keep our society fair and just. Imagine it like a big game where everyone has to follow certain rules to make sure things run smoothly. People in this industry, like lawyers and judges, work to make sure everyone plays by these rules. They might help someone who’s been treated unfairly or make sure a new law is clear and fair. It's a bit like being a referee in a sports game, making sure everyone knows the rules and follows them.

In the legal industry, there are many important skills that can be useful in other areas, like tech. For example, active listening is crucial because you need to understand what people are saying to help them effectively. Adaptability is important because laws and situations can change, and you need to be ready to adjust. Advocacy is about standing up for what’s right, which is useful in any job where you need to support others. Attention to detail helps in catching small but important things, and critical thinking is key for solving problems. These skills, along with teamwork and communication, are valuable in tech and many other fields, helping you work well with others and tackle challenges creatively.
column3:
Here are some examples of job positions in this industry:

As a lawyer, you get to be like a superhero for justice! Lawyers help people understand their rights and fight for them in court. Whether it's helping someone who feels they've been treated unfairly or defending someone accused of a crime, lawyers are there to make sure everyone gets a fair chance.

Being a judge is like being the referee in a big game. Judges listen to both sides of a story and make important decisions to ensure fairness. They help resolve conflicts and make sure everyone follows the rules, keeping our communities safe and orderly.

A paralegal is like a detective who helps lawyers gather all the clues they need to win a case. They do research, organize important documents, and make sure everything is ready for court. Paralegals are essential team players who help lawyers do their best work.

The marketing industry is all about connecting people with products or services they might need or want. Imagine you're at a school fair, and there are different booths trying to get your attention. Each booth is like a company, and the people running them are marketers. Their job is to make their booth look exciting and interesting so you'll stop by and learn more. They might use colorful posters, fun games, or even free samples to catch your eye. In the real world, marketers use things like advertisements, social media, and events to tell people about what they have to offer and why it's great.

In marketing, there are many soft skills that are super useful and can be used in other areas too. For example, active listening is important because marketers need to understand what people want and need. Adaptability helps them change their strategies if something isn't working. Creativity is key for coming up with new and exciting ideas to grab people's attention. Teamwork and collaboration are essential because marketers often work in groups to come up with the best plans. And storytelling is a big part of marketing because it helps create a connection with the audience by telling a story about the product or service. These skills are not just for marketing; they can help in any job where you need to work with others and come up with creative solutions.

Here are some examples of job positions in this industry:

As a social media manager, you get to be the voice of a brand on platforms like Instagram and TikTok. It's like being the person who makes sure everyone knows about your lemonade stand by posting fun pictures and videos, responding to comments, and keeping followers excited about what's coming next.

As a graphic designer, you create eye-catching visuals that grab people's attention. Imagine designing a super cool poster for your lemonade stand that makes everyone want to stop by and try a glass. You get to use colors, fonts, and images to make products look amazing and stand out.

As a market researcher, you become a detective who figures out what people like and want. It's like asking your friends what flavors of lemonade they love the most and then using that information to make the best lemonade ever. You help companies understand their customers better so they can offer products that people really want.

The medical industry is all about helping people stay healthy and feel better when they're sick. It's like a big team of superheroes, but instead of capes, they wear lab coats and stethoscopes. Doctors, nurses, and other healthcare workers work together to diagnose illnesses, treat patients, and even perform surgeries. They use their knowledge of the human body and medicine to make sure everyone gets the care they need. This industry is super important because it helps people live longer, healthier lives.

In the medical industry, there are many soft skills that are important and can be used in other areas too. As an aircraft mechanic, you get to work on airplanes and make sure they're safe to fly. It's like being a doctor for planes, checking their engines and parts to keep them in top shape. You'll learn all about how these amazing machines work and help keep the skies safe for everyone.

User Experience Design, often called UX Design, is all about making sure that when people use a product, like a website or an app, they have a smooth and enjoyable experience. Imagine playing your favorite video game or using a fun app on your phone. UX designers work behind the scenes to make sure everything is easy to use and looks great. They think about how people will interact with the product and try to make it as user-friendly as possible. This means they might change the layout, colors, or even the way you navigate through the app to make sure it feels just right.

In the world of UX Design, there are many soft skills that are super important and can be used in other jobs too. For example, active listening is crucial because designers need to understand what users want and need. Adaptability helps them adjust to new challenges or changes in a project. Empathy allows them to put themselves in the users' shoes to create better experiences. Collaboration and teamwork are key because designers often work with other people to bring ideas to life. Skills like problem-solving and critical thinking help them find creative solutions to design challenges. These skills are not just useful in UX Design but can be valuable in any job you might have in the future!
column3:
Here are some examples of job positions in this industry:

As a UX Designer, you get to be the mastermind behind making apps and websites super easy and fun to use. Imagine designing a game where every button is in the perfect spot, so players can focus on having fun instead of figuring out how to play. You’ll be like a detective, figuring out what users need and making sure they have the best experience possible.

A UX Researcher is like a scientist who studies how people interact with technology. You’ll get to talk to users, watch how they use apps or websites, and gather all sorts of cool data. This helps designers understand what works and what doesn’t, so they can create even better products. It’s like being a secret agent, uncovering the mysteries of what makes a great user experience.

As a UX Writer, your job is to make sure all the words in an app or website are clear and helpful. You’ll write the text for buttons, menus, and instructions, making sure users know exactly what to do. It’s like being a storyteller, but instead of writing books, you’re crafting the perfect words to guide people through their digital adventures.

User Experience Research, often called UX Research, is all about understanding how people use products, like apps or websites, and making those products better and easier to use. Imagine you're playing a video game, and you find a level that's really hard to understand. UX researchers would study how you and others play that level to figure out what's confusing. They might watch you play, ask you questions, or even have you try out new versions of the game to see if it's more fun. Their job is to make sure that using technology feels natural and enjoyable, just like hanging out with friends or playing your favorite sport.

In the world of UX Research, there are many soft skills that are super important and can be used in lots of other jobs too. For example, active listening is crucial because researchers need to really hear what users are saying to understand their needs. Adaptability helps them adjust to new information or changes in a project. Empathy is key because it allows researchers to put themselves in the users' shoes and understand their feelings. Collaboration and teamwork are important because UX researchers often work with designers and developers to create the best possible product. These skills help UX researchers not only in their field but also in any job where understanding people and working well with others is important.
column3:
Here are some examples of job positions in this industry:

As a UX Research Assistant, you get to be the sidekick to experienced researchers. You help them gather information by setting up interviews or surveys with people who use apps or websites. It's like being a detective's apprentice, learning how to uncover clues about what makes technology fun and easy to use.

A Usability Tester is someone who gets to try out new apps or games before anyone else. Your job is to play around with them and see if anything is confusing or doesn't work well. It's like being a game tester, but for all kinds of technology, and your feedback helps make these products better for everyone.

As a UX Interviewer, you talk to people about their experiences using different gadgets or apps. You ask them questions to find out what they like or what frustrates them. It's like being a friendly reporter, gathering stories that help designers create amazing and user-friendly technology.
